articles.title: 5.5 dan 7.0 gacha 90 kunda: har bir o'quvchimga beradigan rejam
articles.author: Maqsudjon Polatov
articles.body:
Ko'pchilik IELTS'ga "umumiy ingliz tili"ni oshirish deb qaraydi. Bu eng katta xato. IELTS — format imtihoni: siz tilni bilishingiz YETARLI bo'lgan holda ham, formatni bilmasangiz 1.0 ball yo'qotasiz.

1-oy — diagnostika va poydevor. Birinchi haftada to'liq mock test topshiring (bepul, flarestamina.com da). Natija sizga qaysi bo'lim eng zaifligini aytadi. Keyin 3 hafta faqat shu bo'limga: har kuni 1 soat, faqat o'sha section turlari. Listening'da xato qilgan joyingizni qayta eshiting — javobni ko'rib emas, nima uchun eshitmaganingizni topib.

2-oy — hajm. Endi har kuni bitta to'liq section + haftada 1 marta to'liq mock. Writing uchun: haftada 3 ta Task 2 essay, lekin uchtasi ham BIR XIL mavzu turida (masalan, opinion). Bir turni to'liq o'zlashtirmay ikkinchisiga o'tmang. Speaking uchun: telefonda o'zingizni yozib oling, keyin eshiting — bu og'riqli, lekin eng tez ishlaydigan usul.

3-oy — imtihon rejimi. Har mock testni REAL vaqtda, tanaffussiz, telefonsiz topshiring. Maqsad: mock'larda barqaror 7.0-7.5 ko'rish. Imtihonga mock'ingiz target'dan 0.5 baland bo'lganda boring — imtihon kuni stress o'rtacha 0.5 ball yeydi.

Eng muhimi: 90 kun davomida HAR KUNI kamida 45 daqiqa. Haftada 1 marta 6 soat o'qigan odam, har kuni 1 soat o'qigan odamdan doim yutqazadi. Til — bu odat, sprint emas.

articles.title: 7 things famous IELTS teachers keep repeating
articles.author: Flarestamina Editorial
articles.body:
We read the blogs and channels of the most-followed IELTS educators — ex-examiners and band 9 teachers — and the same advice keeps appearing. Here are the seven points they all agree on.

1. Answer the question, not the topic. Simon (ex-examiner, ielts-simon.com) built his whole method on this: Task 2 essays lose bands not for grammar, but for drifting off the exact question asked.

2. In Writing, ideas beat vocabulary. Examiners reward a clear, well-developed argument written in simple English over a weak argument dressed in memorized "academic" phrases. Memorized templates are easy to spot and score poorly.

3. In Speaking, fluency beats accuracy. Hesitating to find the perfect word costs more than a small grammar slip. Keep talking; self-correct naturally.

4. In Listening, predict before you hear. Read the questions in the preparation time and guess what type of word fits each gap (a number? a name? a noun?). This single habit is worth half a band.

5. In Reading, the answers come in order (mostly). For most question types, answer locations follow the passage order — use this to stop re-reading from the top.

6. Do full tests under real timing. Every serious teacher says the same: untimed practice builds knowledge, timed practice builds the score.

7. Review mistakes deeper than you practice. One test fully analysed — why each wrong answer was wrong — is worth three tests done and forgotten.

All seven work only with volume: real, full-format practice. That's exactly what Flarestamina gives you for free — 100+ tests in the real exam format.
